title: Chinese Currency Accountability Act of 2025
stage_label: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Foreign Relations.
plain_summary: This law says the United States must vote against giving China's money more power in a special international money system run by the International Monetary Fund. The US can only vote yes if China follows all the rules, stops manipulating its currency, and agrees to work fairly with other countries on loans and trade credits. The International Monetary Fund uses a basket of different countries' money to create special reserve money that countries can use in emergencies. China has to prove it is playing by international financial rules before its currency can get more influence in this system. This law will stop working automatically after ten years.
last_action_text: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Foreign Relations.
subjects: International monetary system and foreign exchange

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Chinese Currency Accountability Act of twenty twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Opposition of the United States to an Increase in the Weight of the Chinese Renminbi in the Special Drawing Rights Basket of the International Monetary Fund
sections.text: The Secretary of the Treasury must tell the US representatives at the International Monetary Fund to vote against giving China's money called the renminbi more importance in a special group of world currencies. The International Monetary Fund is a group of countries that work together on money issues. Special Drawing Rights are like a worldwide form of money made up of different countries' currencies mixed together. The US must vote against increasing China's share unless the Secretary of the Treasury writes a report to two committees in Congress. The report must prove three things. First, China must be following all the rules it agreed to follow as a member of the International Monetary Fund. Second, in the past twelve months, the US government has not found that China has been unfairly controlling the value of its money to help its own trade. Third, China must be following the rules of two groups called the Paris Club and the OECD Arrangement on Officially Supported Export Credits, which are about how countries lend money to each other and support their businesses when selling things to other countries.

sections.title: Section 3 Sunset
sections.text: Section two of this law will stop working ten years after this law is passed.
